I agree with the initial conclusion that option B is correct because the evidence confirms the mylohyoid muscle is innervated by the fifth nerve. Furthermore, the detailed anatomy texts now clarify that the anterior belly of the digastric muscle is entirely innervated by CN V, and the tensor tympani muscle is also correctly attributed to this nerve. No contradictory evidence has emerged that would challenge these well-established connections. Hence, selecting B continues to represent the current consensus view, grounded in substantial scientific evidence corroborated across reputable anatomical references.
